title: MitM Lab Exercise
when_to_use: During internal pentests when MitM is in scope

steps.title: Initial Setup/Scanning
steps.instruction: Enable IP forwarding on attacker machine to intercept and relay traffic
steps.tip: Start with the most reliable information source for mitm lab exercise
steps.expected_output: Confirmed IP forwarding enabled allowing traffic to pass through attacker system

steps.title: Data Collection/Configuration
steps.instruction: Execute ARP spoofing attack to position attacker between victim and gateway
steps.tip: Organize data systematically to avoid duplicates and ensure coverage
steps.expected_output: Confirmed ARP spoofing active with victim traffic routing through attacker

steps.title: Analysis/Enumeration
steps.instruction: Capture network traffic using Wireshark to analyze protocols and data
steps.tip: Look for patterns and correlations in collected data
steps.expected_output: Packet capture containing victim traffic showing unencrypted communications

steps.title: Advanced Technique/Filtering
steps.instruction: Filter captured traffic to identify credentials in HTTP, FTP, and Telnet protocols
steps.tip: Use multiple techniques to increase coverage and accuracy
steps.expected_output: Extracted plaintext credentials from unencrypted network communications

steps.title: Correlation/Testing
steps.instruction: Use Bettercap as alternative framework for advanced MitM attack orchestration
steps.tip: Validate findings across different tools and methods
steps.expected_output: Confirmed successful attack using Bettercap with captured credentials

steps.title: Validation/Exploitation
steps.instruction: Run Responder to capture NTLMv2 hashes and credentials from network services
steps.expected_output: Captured NTLM hashes from authentication attempts and NTLMv1/v2 credentials

steps.title: Documentation/Reporting
steps.instruction: Analyse all captured data and document security implications
steps.tip: Review all findings before compilation for accuracy and completeness
steps.expected_output: Report detailing intercepted credentials, protocols used, and security findings
